prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis for pyridine was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (PySCF backend). The calculation was carried out in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1, using the geometry provided in `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`.

The calculated Fukui indices for each atom are as follows:

| Atom Index (0-based) | Symbol | Fukui f+ (Electrophilic Attack) | Fukui f- (Nucleophilic Attack) | Dual Descriptor (f+ - f-) |
|:---------------------|:-------|:------------------------------|:-------------------------------|:--------------------------|
| 0                    | N      | 0.48422572                    | 0.064122671                    | 0.42010305                |
| 1                    | C      | 0.32447226                    | 0.0061877145                   | 0.31828455                |
| 2                    | C      | 0.14032091                    | 0.11823139                     | 0.022089525               |
| 3                    | C      | 0.14039587                    | 0.1182626                      | 0.022133269               |
| 4                    | C      | 0.29962627                    | 0.11206595                     | 0.18756031                |
| 5                    | C      | 0.29947707                    | 0.11200985                     | 0.18746722                |
| 6                    | H      | -0.14151319                   | 0.08284851                     | -0.2243617                |
| 7                    | H      | -0.1336841                    | 0.098179487                    | -0.23186359               |
| 8                    | H      | -0.13365905                   | 0.09818226                     | -0.23184131               |
| 9                    | H      | -0.1398163                    | 0.094960093                    | -0.23477639               |
| 10                   | H      | -0.13984545                   | 0.094949479                    | -0.23479493               |

The most electrophilic site (highest Fukui f+) is the Nitrogen atom (atom index 0) with a Fukui f+ value of 0.48422572.
The most nucleophilic site (highest Fukui f-) is the Carbon atom at index 3 with a Fukui f- value of 0.1182626.
